text: The moon follows our car because it wants to come home with us.
context: Said by a 4-year-old during a night drive

text: When you die, your bones become the ground and then flowers grow from you.
context: Said by a 5-year-old after visiting a cemetery

text: Dogs can't look up because their eyes are too heavy.
context: Said by a 6-year-old explaining dogs to a friend

text: Spaghetti is just tired noodles.
context: Said by a 4-year-old at dinner

text: The sun goes to sleep in the ocean. That's why the water is warm.
context: Said by a 5-year-old at the beach

text: If you mix all the colors together you get black because they're all fighting.
context: Said by a 6-year-old during art class

text: My brain has a tiny man inside who tells me ideas but sometimes he's sleeping.
context: Said by a 5-year-old explaining why he couldn't think of an answer

text: Grandpa is really old. He was probably alive when they invented trees.
context: Said by a 4-year-old about his grandfather

text: Fish don't close their eyes because the water would get inside their head.
context: Said by a 5-year-old at an aquarium

text: I think clouds are made of pillow stuffing that escaped.
context: Said by a 4-year-old looking out the window

text: People used to be small and then the food made them big. That's called growing up.
context: Said by a 5-year-old explaining life to his baby sister

text: The wind is just the sky breathing.
context: Said by a 6-year-old on a windy day

text: You can't see air but you can feel it so it's kind of like a ghost but not scary.
context: Said by a 5-year-old during a science lesson

text: Shadows are afraid of the dark. That's why they only come out when it's light.
context: Said by a 6-year-old playing outside

text: When it rains, that means the clouds are too full and they spill.
context: Said by a 4-year-old watching rain

text: I want to be a doctor so I can see what bones look like because I've never seen mine.
context: Said by a 5-year-old at career day

text: If the earth is spinning, how come my juice doesn't fall off the table?
context: Said by a 6-year-old at breakfast

text: My cat doesn't talk because she already knows everything.
context: Said by a 4-year-old about her pet

text: Maybe aliens are just people who live really far away and don't have cars.
context: Said by a 7-year-old watching a space documentary

text: Salad is just crunchy water with leaves.
context: Said by a 5-year-old refusing to eat dinner

text: The refrigerator hums because it doesn't know the words.
context: Said by a 4-year-old in the kitchen

text: Time goes slower when you're waiting because it knows you're watching it.
context: Said by a 7-year-old waiting for Christmas

text: Butterflies are just flowers that got bored of sitting still.
context: Said by a 5-year-old in the garden

text: I don't like the dark because that's when my room forgets what it looks like.
context: Said by a 4-year-old at bedtime

text: Water doesn't have a taste because it's everybody's favorite and you can't pick a flavor for everyone.
context: Said by a 6-year-old at the dinner table
